what_it_does: Defines CommandPalette, a Cmd+K overlay that builds a unified searchable index across canvases, actions, every icon in the library, base colors, and voice rules, then fuzzy-filters and ranks results by query, supports keyboard navigation (arrows/enter/esc), and runs each item's action (navigate, copy a name/hex, open export). Exposed on window.CommandPalette.
observations: Index combines: 12 canvases (id/label/icon), 3 actions, all keys of window.CV_ICONS.data, 8 base colors with hex literals, and 7 voice rules
Scoring: exact label match 100, startsWith 80, includes 50, other-field match 20; results capped at 40 and sorted
Keyboard handler bound to window while open (Esc/ArrowDown/ArrowUp/Enter); selection resets on query change; input auto-focused after 30ms
Renders items with an icon glyph, color swatch, or fallback CvIcon depending on item type; shows kind tag and ↵ on the active row
Footer shows nav hints and 'N of total' count
Entirely inline-styled overlay (modal scrim, blur, card) using CSS var tokens plus a few raw px
Base colors and the scrim color (rgba(31,26,18,.35)) are hardcoded hex
standouts: One unified index spanning structural navigation AND content entities (icons, colors, voice rules) — the palette searches the design system's contents, not just routes
Pulls the live icon set from window.CV_ICONS.data so the icon search reflects the actual library
conventions: Window-global component export pattern; uses CvIcon and CSS var tokens

Breaks single-source: the 8 base colors are re-listed with literal hex here (and the scrim uses raw rgba) rather than read from the token registry — and the 7 voice rules are re-listed inline rather than from voice.conceptv
concerns: Color hex values (gold #E0C010, canvas #FBF7EC, dark #1F1A12, etc.) and voice rules are duplicated inline — a second home for values the project mandates be single-sourced; if the tokens change, this list drifts
Scrim color rgba(31,26,18,.35) is a raw literal rather than a token
notes: The Cmd+K command palette — a fuzzy-ranked unified search over canvases, actions, all library icons, base colors, and voice rules; note the colors and voice rules are hardcoded here rather than sourced from the registries.
questions: Why aren't the base colors and voice rules read from the token/voice registries that the project treats as single sources?
Where is CV_ICONS.data populated and is its key set the canonical icon name list?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — comment says a Cmd+K cross-canvas search overlay and the file builds exactly that, indexing more than canvases (icons/colors/voice/actions).
apparent_themes: Unified search/index over the whole system
Projection of the system's contents into a searchable surface
intention_signals: Aims to give one fast keyboard-driven entry point to everything in the studio — jump to any canvas, copy any icon name or color, recall any voice rule — by indexing the system's own contents into a ranked palette.
novelty: Indexing content entities (icons, colors, voice rules) alongside navigation/actions in the same Cmd+K palette, with the icon set drawn live from the library.
contribution: Contributes the cross-canvas command-palette surface: a unified fuzzy-ranked index of canvases, actions, live library icons, base colors, and voice rules with full keyboard control.
summary_for_embedding: A Cmd+K command-palette React component building a fuzzy-ranked unified search index over canvases, actions, live library icons, base colors, and voice rules with keyboard navigation, on window.CommandPalette.
intention_for_embedding: To provide one fast keyboard-driven entry point to navigate canvases and recall/copy the design system's own contents (icons, colors, voice rules) from a single searchable palette.
